meta.purpose: Replay fixture for the two PHASE-46 reviewProject false positives (in-memory Notes API). Used by scripts/spikes/phase47_fp_replay.js to prove (real gpt-4o, owner-gated) that reviewer_v6 + security_auditor_v7 no longer emit the spurious BLOCKERs.
meta.captured_from_real_run: PHASE-46 gated validation 2026-06-30 (Notes-API leg, real gpt-4o)
meta.ground_truth: All 5 review BLOCKERs are false-positives / over-fires; the build is correct (8/8 L5b PASS; GET/:id, PUT, DELETE all return 404 on missing; DELETE checks the affected result; storage is an in-memory Map with NO SQL sink).

reviewer_blockers: No check on DELETE to verify if a note was actually deleted.
GET /notes should return a 404 if a note is not found by id.
No validation of request query parameters

spec.scope: The system is an in-memory Notes API designed to manage personal notes with capabilities for creating, updating, retrieving, and deleting notes. Each note includes a title, body, category, and tags. The API can list all notes, apply optional category filters, and perform keyword searches across the title and body fields.

decisions.decision: Use Express.js to create and manage the API routes.
decisions.rationale: Express provides a straightforward and efficient way to create RESTful APIs.

decisions.decision: Store notes in a JavaScript Map object within the server's memory.
decisions.rationale: A Map offers efficient key-value storage and is well-suited for the small data scale of this application.

decisions.decision: Generate unique note IDs as sequential integers starting from 1.
decisions.rationale: Sequential integers provide a simple, efficient means of ID generation suitable for small-scale, in-memory datasets.

decisions.decision: Implement a validation middleware to ensure title length is no more than 200 characters.
decisions.rationale: Validation of input ensures data integrity and prevents invalid data from being stored.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /notes creates a new note, assigning a sequential integer ID, validating title length (max 200 chars), and storing the note with title, body, category, and tags.
GET /notes retrieves all notes, optionally filtered by ?category=<category> or searched with ?q=<keyword> against title and body.
GET /notes/:id returns a single note by ID, or a 404 error if the note is not found.
PUT /notes/:id updates an existing note by ID after validating input, or returns a 404 error if the note is not found.
DELETE /notes/:id removes a note by ID, returns a 204 status on success, or a 404 error if the note is not found.

files_to_create.purpose: Initializes the Express server, mounts all API routes, and sets the app to listen on the specified port.
Defines the RESTful routes for note operations, including CRUD and list with filter/search capabilities.
Contains middleware for validating incoming request data, particularly ensuring the title does not exceed a 200 character limit.
Implements the in-memory data storage using a JavaScript Map for storing and retrieving notes.
Contains the function for generating sequential integer IDs starting from 1 for new notes.

out_of_scope: Concurrency management or locking beyond basic in-memory operations

design.design_summary: The system is an in-memory Notes API for managing personal notes, with capabilities for creating, updating, retrieving, and deleting notes. Each note consists of a title, body, category, and tags, and the API supports listing notes with optional category filtering and keyword search across title and body.

components.name: ServerEntry
components.purpose: Initializes the server, mounts all API routes, and listens on the specified port.

components.name: InMemoryDataStore
components.purpose: Stores notes in-memory with fast access by note ID.

components.name: NotesRouter
components.purpose: Defines RESTful routes for handling note operations (CRUD and list with filter/search).

components.name: IDGenerator
components.purpose: Generates sequential integer IDs starting from 1 for new notes.

components.name: ValidationMiddleware
components.purpose: Validates incoming request data for notes, ensuring required constraints are met.

design.data_flow: Clients interact with the Notes API through HTTP requests. POST /notes generates a new note ID, validates the title (max 200 chars), and stores the note in the InMemoryDataStore. GET /notes retrieves all notes, applying a ?category= filter or ?q= keyword search if specified. GET /notes/:id fetches a single note by ID, returning 404 if not found. PUT /notes/:id updates an existing note after validating input, with 404 if the note does not exist. DELETE /notes/:id removes a note by ID, returning 204 on success or 404 if not found.

technology_choices.rationale: Express provides a lightweight and flexible framework suitable for building RESTful APIs quickly.
Maps offer efficient in-memory storage and retrieval by keys (note IDs), which is ideal for in-memory persistence.
Sequential integers are simple and effective for uniquely identifying records in a small, in-memory system.

integration_points.name: Notes API
integration_points.notes: REST endpoints for note manipulation: POST /notes, GET /notes, GET /notes/:id, PUT /notes/:id, DELETE /notes/:id.

identified_risks.risk: In-memory data loss on server restart
identified_risks.mitigation: Document the transient nature of data and consider options for persistence if future enhancements require it.

identified_risks.risk: Scalability limits due to in-memory storage
identified_risks.mitigation: Clearly specify usage limits and scale considerations; switch to a database in future iterations if necessary.

identified_risks.risk: Concurrent request handling leading to race conditions
identified_risks.mitigation: Handle race conditions explicitly in JavaScript and use locking mechanisms if the application grows in complexity.
